details: A credential-harvesting package distributed as a trojanized npm module. The postinstall hook (install.js) runs automatically on installation and collects: environment variables (Coinbase API keys/secrets, Supabase service-role keys, OpenAI API keys, AWS credentials, Revolut API secrets, JWT secrets, mnemonic phrases, database URLs, and any env containing PRIVATE_KEY/SECRET/TOKEN);.env files from the package directory and two parent levels; SSH private keys from ~/.ssh/; the full ~/.npmrc file; ~/.aws/credentials; and git config output. All stolen data is exfiltrated via HTTPS POST to webhook[.]site/22e20640-e2a1-4bb2-b203-061077d055ff. Errors are silently suppressed so the install appears to succeed normally.
